star_log_final.reported_percent_validation: each fixed two-decimal STAR percentage must agree with its exact count fraction within 0.00005 fraction; public fractions derive from counts

unaccepted_fraction.display_name: STAR unaccepted (too-many-loci or unmapped) template fraction

featurecounts_summary.paired_end_semantic: -p declares paired-end input; absence of --countReadPairs preserves individual-read counting

trimgalore_cli_source.semantic: --fastqc_args is an optional CLI value documented to imply --fastqc

trimgalore_main_source.semantic: both single-end and paired-end routes run bundled FastQC when fastqc_args is present

sample_status_filtering.fastqc_route_semantics: skip_fastqc gates the separate raw FASTQC process and later filtered FASTQC process; when trimming is enabled with Trim Galore, the pinned module config always supplies --fastqc_args and the Trim Galore process publishes its bundled post-trim HTML/ZIP independently of skip_fastqc
sample_status_filtering.behavior: fastp status admits only fixed 1.0.1 reports whose bounded duplicate-free JSON has consistent before/after reads and bases and passed_filter_reads equal to after_filtering.total_reads; Trim Galore status reconstructs the upstream binary32 Float(total)-Float(removed) route from equal retained FastQC mate counts and fully validated fixed Cutadapt 4.0 rows; STAR status requires the complete validated Log.final.out before its printed unique percent is admitted; every status/native-evidence path and absence is SHA-snapshotted at reconciliation and revalidated after candidate collection

rseqc_tin.behavior: tin.py applies a case-sensitive global basename.replace('bam', '') while the pinned nf-core wrapper expects bam.baseName outputs
rseqc_tin.script_semantic_difference: sdist and wheel scripts differ only in their shebang
rseqc_tin.standard_deviation: numpy.std population standard deviation with default ddof=0 over TIN values theoretically bounded from 0 through 100; enforce mean/median <= 100 and stdev <= 50 after the public 12-decimal canonicalization envelope

star_log_final.behavior: Log.final.out separates mapped-to-too-many-loci from its three unmapped categories and counts one paired-end template per read pair

featurecounts_summary.behavior: the pinned module supplies -p for paired-end input without --countReadPairs, so summary values count individual reads

salmon_meta_info.behavior: num_processed and num_mapped are emitted from numObservedFragments and numMappedFragments

public_exclusions.reason: MultiQC 1.33 embeds analysis-file paths unless a future pinned runtime gate proves a privacy override
contains command and absolute path fields
contains source paths
contains execution-private diagnostics
unbounded dynamic schema
unbounded generated report text
contains invocation details
contains execution-private diagnostics
contains command-line parameters and input filename fields
HTML content is not covered by the fixed machine-data privacy audit
contains invocation diagnostics
contains staged database and execution diagnostics
contains staged database and execution diagnostics
contains a samtools command line
contains the featureCounts command and staged paths
opaque serialized R object is outside the audited public contract
raw Picard metrics may contain command and staged-path headers
contains execution environment details
unreviewed generated scripts and raw logs are not public artifacts
contains command and path details
nondeterministic name and absolute input paths
contains absolute input paths
outside results root and contains private runtime state
outside results root and contains private runtime state

explicit_profile_policy.fastqc_route_semantics: raw and post-filter FastQC follow skip_fastqc; Trim Galore bundled post-trim FastQC is required whenever trimming is enabled with trimmer=trimgalore, including skip_fastqc=true
explicit_profile_policy.trimgalore_metrics: trimmed_fastqc_total_sequences_is_exact_final_retained_reads; fixed MultiQC Cutadapt rows have exact 9-column shape, Cutadapt 4.0 identity, bounded integer fields, r_written equal to r_processed because the product forbids discard-untrimmed, count/base range invariants, and percent_trimmed agreement within 0.000000001 percentage points; exact final retained bases require a future sanitized derivative
explicit_profile_policy.sample_threshold_filtering: fixed MultiQC status rows never independently shrink the output matrix: fastp 1.0.1 JSON is duplicate-key rejecting and bounded by the shared JSON limits, and its passed_filter_reads must equal after_filtering.total_reads while retained reads/bases cannot exceed before_filtering; Trim Galore rows must reproduce Float(input)-Float(removed) from fully validated Cutadapt rows and equal retained FastQC mate counts; mapping rows require the complete fixed STAR Log.final.out grammar, six-way count partition, count-percent agreement, and exact printed unique percent; a trim equality row remains execution-passing, only a strict trim count below threshold shrinks analysis outputs, and disabled routes, missing evidence, stale tables, contradictory rows, or observable evidence replacement fail closed
explicit_profile_policy.umi_discard_read_layout: a discarded mate makes the post-extraction route single-end while raw FastQC and saved UMI-extracted reads retain the authored paired layout
explicit_profile_policy.rseqc_csi: inner_distance, read_distribution, and tin are removed exactly as defineQcTools does for bam_csi_index
explicit_profile_policy.multiqc_sample_identity: sample identifiers containing any of the 159 identifier-alphabet literals cleaned by MultiQC 1.33 defaults or the pinned nf-core config are reserved; when MultiQC is enabled, canonical IDs, authored-PE aliases, effective downstream aliases, and FASTQ simpleNames after prepended extra_fn_clean_exts, default fn_clean_exts, fn_clean_trim, and global exact replacement must have one sample-and-mate-role owner before execution
explicit_profile_policy.rseqc_tin_sample_identity: effective TIN rejects case-sensitive lowercase bam in sample IDs before workspace planning; authored IDs are never rewritten

multiqc_sample_identity_sources.general_stats_grouping: exact per-sample union of canonical S and, only on fixed paired routes with authored raw FastQC or paired Trim Galore evidence, grouped S Read 1 and S Read 2 rows; threshold failures do not remove pre-threshold identities

ownership_policies.general_stats: exact per-sample union of canonical S plus paired grouped rows S Read 1 and S Read 2 when authored raw FastQC is enabled or paired Trim Galore evidence exists before threshold filtering
ownership_policies.cutadapt: exact effective-downstream identities for every sample
ownership_policies.fastp: exact canonical identities for every sample
ownership_policies.fastqc_raw: exact authored-layout identities for every sample
ownership_policies.fastqc_trimmed_trimgalore: exact effective-downstream identities for every sample
ownership_policies.fastqc_trimmed_fastp: exact effective-downstream identities for analysis-pass samples
ownership_policies.fastqc_filtered: exact effective-downstream identities for analysis-pass samples

published_multiqc_table_audit.status_tables: separately parsed and reconciled against bounded native evidence; never admitted by the generic row audit; both status tables and every native evidence path including absence are content-SHA snapshotted at reconciliation
published_multiqc_table_audit.revalidation: content_sha256_snapshot_at_reconciliation_and_complete_descriptor_relative_reread_after_candidate_collection; any status or evidence replacement changes the result to a stable race failure

determinism.unknown_output_policy: audit fixed sample-bearing namespaces with explicit bounded single-level scans and audit every published MultiQC machine table with descriptor-relative bounded content ownership checks; reject foreign identities, ownership collisions, duplicate rows, malformed content, recognized non-regular entries, and observable replacement; ignore unrecognized names and outputs outside the enumerated namespaces
